title: Gel DNA Extraction
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1bj38","text":"This protocol details how to extract DNA from agarose gel using Thermo Scientific GeneJET Gel Extraction Kit. This is suitable for gels run in either TAE or TBE buffer, and for DNA fragments from 25 bp to 20 kb. The extraction is performed using spin columns.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":64,"length":44}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"C8EC8C80505511EBAF65FDEAFEC5C60D","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/K0691#/K0691"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"d76g3","text":"When performing gel excision, use necessary precautions to avoid tissue damage due to UV radiation exposure. If a proper shield is not in place, wear a full face covering made of a UV protective material, such as polycarbonate.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"12h0t","text":"GeneJET Gel Extraction Kit","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":26}],"data":{}},{"key":"jh45","text":"1.5 mL centrifuge tube(s)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ofbs","text":"[May need]   sodium acetate,   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":11,"length":1},{"key":2,"offset":29,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"3a6lr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"2022C9E0506211EBAF65FDEAFEC5C60D","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/K0691#/K0691"}},"1":{"type":"concentration","mutability":"","data":{"concentration":"3","guid":"48641DF0506211EBAF65FDEAFEC5C60D","id":0,"label":"","unit":21}},"2":{"type":"ph","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"52E30980506211EBAF65FDEAFEC5C60D","id":0,"number":"5.2"}}}}
steps: Perform Gel Electrophoresis on your DNA sample to separate the DNA bands. The protocol recommends 1 % w/v for TBE/agarose gel and 1.2 % w/v for TAE/agarose gel. Recommended well depth is 1 mm / 1 μL sample. Other recommendations can be found in the original protocol, such as recommended voltage.
Pre-weigh and mark 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes for each DNA band.
Weigh the tubes with gel, and determine gel mass.

Place gel in transilluminator, and using a clean scalpel or blade, closely excise the desired DNA band(s). To avoid damage to the transilluminator surface, use a blunted blade. Each extraction column can process up to 1 g 1 agarose gel.
Add 100 µL Binding Buffer per 100 mg gel to centrifuge tube with gel (1:1 volume:weight). For gels with greater than 2 % w/v agarose, use 2:1 volume:weight.
Incubate gel mixture at 55 °C until gel is completely dissolved. Agitate by inversion occasionally to improve mixing. Ensure that gel is completely dissolved before proceeding.
Check color of solution. A yellow color is desired. If color is orange or violet, add 10 µL of 3 Molarity (M) sodium acetate, pH 5.2 . Mix well.

If DNA fragment length is ≤500 bp, add 1 gel volume of 100 % volume isopropanol to gel solution. E.g. 100 µL isopropanol to 100 mg gel in 100 µL binding buffer. Mix well. If DNA fragment length is >10 kb, add 1 gel volume water to gel solution. Mix well.
Transfer up to 800 µL gel solution to extraction column. If gel solution volume exceeds 800 µL , add solution to column in stages; centrifuge for 1 min each time, discarding flow

Centrifuge at 14000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 . Always make a counter-balance when centrifuging. This is best achieved by weighing the tube and using water to make another tube of equal weight. Place same-weight pairs on opposite sides of centrifuge. A trick for getting the weight right quickly is to place tube with gel on balance, tare it, add empty tube to balance. Add 1 µL per 1 mg difference.
Discard flow through, place column back in same collection tube.
Add 700 µL wash buffer to purification column.

Centrifuge at 14000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 . Discard flow through. Place column back in same collection tube. Be sure to make a new counter balance for this step and all later steps since the weights will change.
Centrifuge empty column at 14000 x g, °C, for an additional 00:01:00 to remove any residual wash buffer. This step is not optional.
Transfer purification column to clean 1.5 mL centrifuge tube.
Add 50 µL of Elution Buffer to the center of the column membrane. An elution volume between 20-50 μL can be used. Do not use less that 10 µL elution volume.
Discard purification column. Store DNA at -20 °C .
creator: Kenneth Schackart
